summary: The prevalence of Molar-Incisor Hypomineralization varies widely but typically falls between approximately 6.2% to 48.8%, aligning most closely with answer choice C based on available data and common reported ranges. This variability reflects differences observed globally without pinpoint accuracy provided specifically by Weerheijm’s work or other highly targeted studies addressing exact figures.
